name: SVG To 3D Forge
short_description: A Mac-first vector forge for scaffolding SVG assets and turning them into local STL, OBJ, or GLB model files with an explicit Blender export step.
audience: Indie makers building icons, badges, or plaques for physical output
Mac users who want a repeatable SVG-to-mesh workflow without cloud tooling
People shaping logos or interface marks into simple 3D assets
detail_lines: Scaffolds simple vector marks, badges, plaques, or keycap-style SVG assets from deterministic local presets before they go near a mesh export.
Uses Blender locally to import SVG curves, extrude them, and export STL, OBJ, or GLB models with explicit depth, bevel, and size controls.
Keeps the workflow honest about what is good for fast prototypes versus what still needs slicer or CAD validation for production geometry.
